commit.message:
v0.2.46: decremental streak loss instead of hard reset

Each missed day reduces streak by 1 (min 0). Formula on completion:
  new_streak = max(0, current_streak - missed_days) + 1
where missed_days = days_since_last_completion - 1.

Updated test: test_streak_decrements_after_gap + test_streak_bottoms_at_zero

commit.message:
fix: remote video preview, transfer execution, transfer dialog labels (#95)

Bug 1 — Remote video preview: setPreviewFile now passes connectionLabel
and provider from activeRemote so the overlay and history panel work.
FilePreviewModal uses downloadRemoteFile blob instead of previewStreamUrl
(which expects a local DB ID that remote browser items don't have).

Bug 2 — Transfer stuck at 0B: enqueueTransferOrFallback now runs
performTransfer as fire-and-forget instead of routing through the
skeleton BullMQ worker. performTransfer already has full Google Drive
logic with DB progress updates and SSE notifications.

Bug 3 — Transfer dialog UUIDs: openBulkPreview resolves connection UUIDs
to human-readable labels via connectionById + getProviderLabel before
passing to BulkOperationPreview.

commit.message:
fix(ast_lowering): use full param span for enum/struct destructuring patterns

When a function parameter is an enum/struct destructuring pattern such as
`Page { title, alerts, posts }`, `get_param_names` correctly lowercased the
struct name to use as the synthesised parameter name (e.g. `page`), but then
only looked for `PatKind::Identifier` spans when computing `source_span`.
Enum patterns fell through to `unwrap_or_default()`, yielding `Span::default()`
(position 0,0), so inlay hints for these parameters were placed at the very
beginning of the file.

Fix by also handling `PatKind::Enum` in the source-span lookup, using the
full `FunctionParameter.span` (which covers the entire pattern including the
closing `}`).  This places the type hint right after the closing brace, which
is the natural position for an annotation following a destructured parameter.

Add a regression test in `tlang_analysis` that verifies no hint from a struct
pattern parameter lands at position (0, 0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Replace BTC-deviation gate with Polymarket share-price direction in SniperStrategy (#24)

* Initial plan

* fix: Replace BTC-deviation gate with Polymarket share-price direction in SniperStrategy

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/zh6025/polymarket-trading-bot/sessions/cdfddf23-5b86-4a0c-bee2-52f2e69b155d

Co-authored-by: zh6025 <211226490+zh6025@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Docker container name conflict on systemd restart

Root cause: systemd service used `docker compose up -d` without proper
cleanup, leaving stale containers behind on stop. On restart, the
container name `/polymarket-bot` was already in use.

Changes:
- Rewrite systemd service to use Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes
  (correct for `docker compose up -d` which detaches immediately)
- Add ExecStartPre=-docker compose down --remove-orphans to clean stale
  containers before starting
- Add ExecStop=docker compose down for proper teardown
- Add ExecReload for rebuild + restart via systemctl reload
- Add Requires=docker.service dependency
- Deploy workflow: add explicit `docker-compose down` before recreate
- DEPLOY.md: fix stale bot_continuous.py reference, add systemd section,
  add container-conflict troubleshooting guide
- DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md: update systemd section to match Docker approach

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/zh6025/polymarket-trading-bot/sessions/ea193015-a41c-4f4b-883c-cf83bb46c15e

Co-authored-by: zh6025 <211226490+zh6025@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix deploy workflow: use /opt/polymarket-bot dir, install systemd service, use docker compose v2

- Deploy workflow now deploys to /opt/polymarket-bot (matches systemd WorkingDirectory)
  instead of ~/polymarket-trading-bot
- Deploy workflow installs/updates the systemd service file on each deployment,
  preventing stale service configs (fixes 217/USER from old User=polymarket)
- Updated docker-compose to docker compose (v2 plugin syntax)
- DEPLOY.md clone path updated to /opt/polymarket-bot for consistency

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/zh6025/polymarket-trading-bot/sessions/f191fd08-2737-47c7-b38e-9cdcd0c2f4e2

Co-authored-by: zh6025 <211226490+zh6025@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Dockerfile CMD: bot_continuous.py → bot_runner.py

The Dockerfile CMD pointed to bot_continuous.py which crashes on startup.
The correct entrypoint is bot_runner.py which handles dry-run simulation
and live trading with proper DirectionScorer + hedge formula logic.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/zh6025/polymarket-trading-bot/sessions/52c25356-d353-4b02-bd70-9e78b64b4cc3

Co-authored-by: zh6025 <211226490+zh6025@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(proxy): Enable weighted load balancing across providers (#13)

* feat(proxy): Enable weighted load balancing across providers

Allow requests to be distributed among multiple upstream providers based
on configurable weights. Add validation to ensure at least one provider
has a positive weight, and update documentation and configuration
examples to clarify usage. This improves scalability and reliability for
deployments with multiple API keys and endpoints.

* feat(proxy): Precompute active providers and optimize streaming buffer

Refactor Gateway to precompute active providers and their total weight at
startup, avoiding per-request allocation and logic in provider selection.
Replace buffer allocation in streaming path with sync.Pool reuse to reduce
memory overhead and improve performance. Remove sha256Pool usage in cache
key generation for simpler, safer hash calculation.

These changes reduce per-request allocation, improving efficiency and
predictability in hot proxy paths, and simplify provider selection logic.

* refactor(config): Remove GetWeight method from ProviderConfig

Simplify code by accessing the Weight field directly instead of using
the GetWeight method. This reduces unnecessary abstraction and makes
field usage more straightforward. The change improves readability and
maintainability since only a simple getter was used.

* Update GEMINI.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(readme): Add weighted providers description and example

Explain provider selection based on weights and how to configure it.
Clarifying this helps users set up load balancing and avoid misconfiguration
that can cause startup failures. Added implementation details for provider
choice in code comments to aid maintainability.

* docs(todo): add tasks for cache correctness with weighted providers

Record new tasks to address potential cache correctness issues now that
provider selection happens per request. This change documents the need to
review cache key scoping and consider including provider identity in the
cache key to prevent erroneous cache hits when multiple providers are used.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: test-cov mise task silently drops CLI args; profile test wrong on 3.14 (#331)

* fix: add usage field to test-cov task and fix testing profile test for Python 3.14

The test-cov mise task silently dropped -m markers because it had no
usage field defined. CI marker expressions like 'not dev_only' were
never applied, causing dev_only/docker/performance tests to run and
fail in CI.

Also fix test_testing_profile_uses_ultralight_constants to account for
HAS_FASTEMBED=False on Python 3.14, where sparse embedding uses
ibm-granite/granite-embedding-30m-sparse instead of qdrant/bm25.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/knitli/codeweaver/sessions/4b9a7c93-cd45-4002-95fa-66e63b8b6981

* fix: eliminate eval in test-cov, parametrize profile test over HAS_FASTEMBED

Address review feedback:
- Replace eval+string-building with POSIX-safe `set --` argument list
  so each arg is passed as its own token (no shell injection risk)
- Parametrize test_testing_profile_uses_ultralight_constants over
  HAS_FASTEMBED=True/False so both backend paths are validated
  deterministically in every test run
- Fix: ST branch reranking assertion now correctly expects
  ULTRALIGHT_ST_RERANKING_MODEL (was asserting fastembed constant)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/knitli/codeweaver/sessions/435f7a0d-d419-41c7-a426-7b93ef59a126

---------

commit.message:
feat: Phase 1 Rust workspace — 5 crates, MCP server, ProcessSandbox

Implements the complete Phase 1 backend:

Crates:
- sandcastle-runtime: SandboxRuntime trait, types (SandboxId, ExecRequest,
  ExecResult, SandboxConfig, ResourceLimits), error types with MCP error codes
- sandcastle-executor: In-sandbox binary, reads JSON from stdin, spawns
  language runtime (python3/node/bash), captures output with timeout/OOM
- sandcastle-manager: Session registry, file validation (allowed_input_dirs,
  path traversal), reaper for expired sessions, oneshot + session execution
- sandcastle-process: ProcessSandbox using libcontainer (youki), OCI spec
  generation via oci-spec crate, namespace isolation (pid/mount/ipc/uts/net),
  pipe-based executor communication, workspace bind-mounts
- sandcastle-server: MCP server using rmcp crate, 6 tools (execute_code,
  create_sandbox, execute_in_session, upload_file, download_file,
  destroy_sandbox), stdio transport, reaper background task

Tests:
- 15 unit tests (9 runtime types, 5 manager with mock runtime, 1 OCI spec)
- Integration test script: executor direct test, timeout handling, MCP server
  tool listing

Tooling:
- scripts/build-rootfs.sh: Docker export rootfs builder for python/bash/node

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Sandcastle — Tiered sandbox execution for AI agents via MCP. Secure code execution with process, gVisor, and Firecracker isolation levels.

Co-authored-by: christaegger-dot <231125860+christaegger-dot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 14: Shimmer Animation (#33)

## Summary

**Phase 14: Shimmer Animation**
**Goal:** Users see a smooth animated skeleton shimmer while translation
is in progress, with no jarring layout shifts
**Status:** Verified ✓ (3/3 must-haves passed)

Adds a polished left-to-right gradient sweep shimmer animation to the
popup loading state. The shimmer plays from translation start until the
result appears, using `.plusLighter` blend mode that adapts to both
light and dark system appearance. Implemented as a zero-layout-impact
`.overlay` ViewModifier to avoid interfering with the popup's
GeometryReader height measurement. Fully respects the Reduce Motion
accessibility setting.

## Changes

### Plan 14-01: ShimmerModifier ViewModifier + Unit Tests
Left-to-right gradient sweep ViewModifier using `.plusLighter` blend
mode with Reduce Motion accessibility fallback.

**Key files created:**
- `Transy/Popup/ShimmerModifier.swift` — ViewModifier with animated
gradient sweep, `.clipped()`, `.allowsHitTesting(false)`
- `TransyTests/ShimmerModifierTests.swift` — 3 structural tests

### Plan 14-02: Wire Shimmer into LoadingPopupText
Connected `ShimmerModifier` to the actual loading UI via `.shimmer()` on
`PopupText` inside `LoadingPopupText.body`.

**Key files modified:**
- `Transy/Popup/PopupView.swift` — Added `.shimmer()` call on
loading-state text

## Requirements Addressed

| ID | Requirement | Status |
|----|------------|--------|
| SHM-01 | Shimmer animation plays on popup text during translation
loading | ✓ |
| SHM-02 | Zero layout impact — no resize notification storm | ✓ |
| SHM-03 | Reduce Motion disables animation — static muted text fallback
| ✓ |

## Verification

- [x] Automated verification: PASSED (3/3 must-haves)
- [x] Human visual verification: gradient sweep, no layout shift, Reduce
Motion compliance
- [x] Unit tests: all pass (`make test`)

## Key Decisions

- `.overlay` architecture (not ZStack) to prevent GeometryReader height
measurement interference
- `.plusLighter` blend mode for light/dark adaptive shimmer appearance
- 1.5s linear `repeatForever(autoreverses: false)` sweep speed
- `.onAppear` for animation start (not `.task`) per pitfall analysis

Closes #28

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace agent-framework metapackage with agent-framework-core

The agent-framework metapackage pulls agent-framework-core[all], which
installs 25+ unused provider packages (pythonnet, boto3, anthropic,
claude-sdk, etc). These cause intermittent ImportError on CI due to
namespace package conflicts in the agent_framework namespace.

Replace with agent-framework-core directly — we only need core, foundry,
openai, and ollama providers. Reduces dependencies from 167 to 81 packages.
